title: SepFrame
description: The JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope for SEP. Every wire message is exactly one of: Request (id + method, a reply is expected), Notification (method, no id, fire-and-forget), SuccessResponse (id + result), or ErrorResponse (id + error). Both peers issue requests over the symmetric channel; each numbers its own `id` space. Per-method `params`/`result` shapes are defined one file each under `methods/`.

Id.description: Request correlation id — an integer or string, unique per sender per connection.

params.description: Method parameters. Shape defined by the method's schema.

Notification.title: SepNotification
Notification.description: A request with no `id`: fire-and-forget, no reply.

result.description: Method result. Shape defined by the method's schema.

id.description: Echoes the request id; null when the offending frame could not be parsed.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code. Standard range plus SEP: -32000 Blocked, -32001 NoUI, -32002 NotTrusted, -32003 ContextViolation, -32004 CapabilityDisabled, -32800 Cancelled.

Context.title: SepContext
Context.description: The two-tier dispatch context carried by every host→ext event/hook/tool/command. Session-mutating actions require the `command` tier or the host rejects them with -32003 ContextViolation.

token.description: Opaque epoch handle; invalidated across reloads so stale contexts can't act.
